name: Channel Guard
description: Restrict who can start new messages (root posts) per channel. Everyone else can only reply within threads. Configure each channel directly with /channel-guard.

settings_schema.header:
**Channel Guard** restricts who can start NEW messages (root posts) per channel. Replies within threads always stay open.

**Two layers of configuration:**

1. **Global settings (below)** — apply to every channel that has Channel Guard enabled.
2. **Per-channel settings** — set inside the channel itself by Channel Admins or System Admins. Use `/channel-guard help` to see the available subcommands.

Example per-channel setup:
```
/channel-guard enable
/channel-guard add @alice @bob
/channel-guard list
```

**Order of evaluation** when a user starts a new message in a restricted channel:

1. Reply inside a thread → always allowed
2. Bot or webhook (if **Always allow bots / webhooks** is on) → allowed
3. System Admin → always allowed
4. Username on the **global always-allowed** list → allowed in every channel
5. Username on the channel's per-channel allow list → allowed
6. Otherwise → blocked and shown the **Default rejection message**
settings_schema.footer: Per-channel allow lists are managed with the `/channel-guard` slash command from inside each channel.

settings.help_text: Usernames that can start new messages in EVERY restricted channel, without needing to be added to each channel's per-channel allow list. Useful for leadership accounts, security teams, or shared comms bots. Comma- or newline-separated, with or without a leading @. Leave empty to rely only on per-channel lists.
Allow bot accounts and incoming webhooks to start new messages regardless of the global or per-channel allow lists. Useful for announcement automation.
Shown to users who are not allowed to start a new message. Markdown is rendered in the ephemeral system message; in the in-channel banner (webapp), only the plain text is shown.
settings.default: Only authorized members can start new messages in this channel. You can still reply within existing threads.
